title: Oscars Betting Odds 2026: Predictions From Ben Zauzmer's Math Winners
text: Ben Zauzmer presents mathematical Oscar predictions for 2026, analyzing matchups between two heavily-nominated films: One Battle After Another and Sinners, which are competing in a record-setting 11 categories. Zauzmer's statistical model factors in earlier award shows, which other categories a film is nominated in, critic scores, betting markets and other quantitative data to calculate win probabilities. The approach assigns greater weight to historically predictive inputs. Best Picture: One Battle After Another leads despite Sinners' record 16 nominations. Zauzmer notes that 44 percent of films to lead the nomination counts did not win best picture, making One Battle After Another's precursor success more decisive. Best Director: Paul Thomas Anderson is heavily favored, potentially earning his first Oscar after 14 career nominations. Best Actor: A competitive three-way race exists between Michael B. Jordan (Sinners), Timothée Chalamet (Marty Supreme), and Wagner Moura (The Secret Agent), with differences of less than one percent. Best Actress: Jessie Buckley (Hamnet) is described as by far the most likely winner, having secured Golden Globe, Critics Choice, BAFTA, and Actor Award wins.
